# moover

`moover` converts movement data into behaviour workflows for animal
scientists.

It is designed to support both:

- beginner-friendly guided workflows
- reproducible scripted workflows from JSON specs

It is designed to work locally on a normal computer, without requiring
access to a high-performance computing (HPC) environment. Larger runs
can still take time, but the goal is to make model building,
optimisation, and prediction accessible to beginners working on their
own machines.

## Installation

Install the development version from GitHub with:

``` r
install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("wobblytwilliams/moover")
```

## What `moover` does

- ingests raw accelerometer data
- converts it to a canonical movement format
- generates epoch features
- trains and optimises Random Forest models
- evaluates models with LOCO metrics and plots
- exports reusable and embedded-friendly model bundles
- predicts behaviour on new datasets using existing bundles

## Core workflows

### 1. Use an existing model

``` r
spec <- moover::create_spec(
  predict = list(model_bundle = "path/to/exported_bundle")
)

moover::run_pipeline(spec, stage = "predict")
```

### 2. Train and export a new model

``` r
spec <- moover::create_spec(
  labels = list(
    tech_file = "tech.csv",
    path = "observations.csv"
  )
)

moover::run_pipeline(spec, stage = "all")
```

### 3. Optimise for embedded deployment

``` r
spec <- moover::create_spec(
  optimise = list(enabled = TRUE)
)

moover::run_pipeline(spec, stage = "all")
```

## Getting started

``` r
moover::init_workspace()
moover::wizard_train()
```

## Documentation

The pkgdown site is intended to be the main tutorial and reference hub:

<https://wobblytwilliams.github.io/moover/>

It includes walkthroughs for:

- setting up folders
- formatting observations
- formatting accelerometer input files
- understanding exported deployment bundles
- building a model
- predicting with an existing model

## Included example data

The package ships with a small example workspace under:

``` r
system.file("extdata", "example_workspace", package = "moover")
```

## TODO

- parquet and arrow ingestion support
- additional model backends beyond Random Forest

# Package index

## Workspace and specs

- [`init_workspace()`](https://wobblytwilliams.github.io/moover/reference/init_workspace.md)
  : Initialise a moover Workspace
- [`create_spec()`](https://wobblytwilliams.github.io/moover/reference/create_spec.md)
  : Create a moover Specification
- [`run_pipeline()`](https://wobblytwilliams.github.io/moover/reference/run_pipeline.md)
  : Run a moover Pipeline

## Guided workflows

- [`wizard_import()`](https://wobblytwilliams.github.io/moover/reference/wizard_import.md)
  : Guided Import Wizard
- [`wizard_train()`](https://wobblytwilliams.github.io/moover/reference/wizard_train.md)
  : Guided Training Wizard
- [`wizard_predict()`](https://wobblytwilliams.github.io/moover/reference/wizard_predict.md)
  : Guided Prediction Wizard

## Core pipeline functions

- [`import_accel()`](https://wobblytwilliams.github.io/moover/reference/import_accel.md)
  : Import Raw Accelerometer Data
- [`build_epoch_features()`](https://wobblytwilliams.github.io/moover/reference/build_epoch_features.md)
  : Build Epoch Features
- [`optimise_model()`](https://wobblytwilliams.github.io/moover/reference/optimise_model.md)
  : Optimise a moover Model
- [`train_model()`](https://wobblytwilliams.github.io/moover/reference/train_model.md)
  : Train a moover Model
- [`export_model()`](https://wobblytwilliams.github.io/moover/reference/export_model.md)
  : Export a moover Model Bundle
- [`predict_behaviour()`](https://wobblytwilliams.github.io/moover/reference/predict_behaviour.md)
  : Predict Behaviour from an Existing Model
- [`load_model_bundle()`](https://wobblytwilliams.github.io/moover/reference/load_model_bundle.md)
  : Load a moover Model Bundle
